papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms. Experiments show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.abstract: We introduce BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers.

papers.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
papers.abstract: Natural language processing tasks, when cast as language modeling, benefit from in-context learning. We demonstrate that language models begin to learn tasks without explicit supervision when trained on large diverse datasets. GPT-2 is a large transformer-based language model with 1.5 billion parameters.

papers.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
papers.abstract: We introduce a unified framework that converts all text-based language problems into a text-to-text format. T5 explores the effectiveness of transfer learning and pre-training at scale, achieving state-of-the-art results on many benchmarks.

papers.title: RoBERTa: A Robustly Optimized BERT Pretraining Approach
papers.abstract: We present a replication study of BERT pretraining that carefully measures the impact of many key hyperparameters and training data size. We find that BERT was significantly undertrained, and can match or exceed the performance of every model published after it.

papers.title: XLNet: Generalized Autoregressive Pretraining for Language Understanding
papers.abstract: XLNet is a generalized autoregressive pretraining method that overcomes the limitations of BERT. By maximizing the expected likelihood over all permutations of the factorization order, XLNet learns bidirectional contexts and avoids relying on corrupted data.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.abstract: We train GPT-3, an autoregressive language model with 175 billion parameters. GPT-3 achieves strong performance on many NLP benchmarks, including translation, question-answering, and cloze tasks, as well as tasks requiring on-the-fly reasoning with few examples.

papers.title: Longformer: The Long-Document Transformer
papers.abstract: Transformer-based models are unable to process long sequences due to their self-attention operation, which scales quadratically with sequence length. We introduce Longformer, a transformer with an attention mechanism that scales linearly with sequence length.

papers.title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
papers.abstract: Transformers suffer from quadratic dependency on sequence length due to their attention mechanism. We propose BigBird, a sparse attention mechanism that reduces this quadratic dependency to linear. BigBird can handle sequences of length up to 8x longer than was previously possible.

papers.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models with Simple and Efficient Sparsity
papers.abstract: We simplify mixture of experts to increase the parameter count of language models while keeping floating point operations per example constant. Switch Transformer scales to over a trillion parameters. We find that scale and sparsity yields better performance across natural language processing tasks.

papers.title: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
papers.abstract: Making language models larger does not inherently make them better at following a user's intent. We train GPT models with human feedback using reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), resulting in InstructGPT. These models are better at following instructions despite having fewer parameters.

papers.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
papers.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets exclusively. LLaMA-13B outperforms GPT-3 on most benchmarks while being smaller.

papers.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
papers.abstract: We develop and release Llama 2, a collection of pretrained and fine-tuned large language models ranging in scale from 7B to 70B parameters. Our fine-tuned models, called Llama 2-Chat, are optimized for dialogue use cases and outperform open-source chat models on most benchmarks.

papers.title: Mistral 7B
papers.abstract: We introduce Mistral 7B, a language model that outperforms Llama 2 13B on all evaluated benchmarks. Mistral 7B uses grouped-query attention for faster inference and sliding window attention to handle sequences of arbitrary length.

papers.title: Mixtral of Experts
papers.abstract: We introduce Mixtral 8x7B, a Sparse Mixture of Experts language model. Mixtral outperforms Llama 2 70B and GPT-3.5 across most benchmarks. Each token has access to 47B parameters but only uses 13B active parameters during inference.
